speaker: TYRREL
text: The tyrannous and bloody act is done,

speaker: TYRREL
text: The most arch deed of piteous massacre

speaker: TYRREL
text: That ever yet this land was guilty of.

speaker: TYRREL
text: Dighton and Forrest, who I did suborn

speaker: TYRREL
text: To do this piece of ruthless butchery,

speaker: TYRREL
text: Albeit they were fleshed villains, bloody dogs,

speaker: TYRREL
text: Melted with tenderness and mild compassion,

speaker: TYRREL
text: Wept like two children in their deaths’ sad story.

speaker: TYRREL
text: “O thus,” quoth Dighton, “lay the gentle babes.”

speaker: TYRREL
text: “Thus, thus,” quoth Forrest, “girdling one another

speaker: TYRREL
text: Within their alabaster innocent arms.

speaker: TYRREL
text: Their lips were four red roses on a stalk,

speaker: TYRREL
text: And in their summer beauty kissed each other.

speaker: TYRREL
text: A book of prayers on their pillow lay,

speaker: TYRREL
text: Which once,” quoth Forrest, “almost changed my mind,

speaker: TYRREL
text: But, O, the devil—” There the villain stopped;

speaker: TYRREL
text: When Dighton thus told on: “We smotherèd

speaker: TYRREL
text: The most replenishèd sweet work of nature

speaker: TYRREL
text: That from the prime creation e’er she framed.”

speaker: TYRREL
text: Hence both are gone with conscience and remorse;

speaker: TYRREL
text: They could not speak; and so I left them both

speaker: TYRREL
text: To bear this tidings to the bloody king.

speaker: TYRREL
text: And here he comes.—All health, my sovereign lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Kind Tyrrel, am I happy in thy news?

speaker: TYRREL
text: If to have done the thing you gave in charge

speaker: TYRREL
text: Beget your happiness, be happy then,

speaker: TYRREL
text: For it is done.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But did’st thou see them dead?

speaker: TYRREL
text: I did, my lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And buried, gentle Tyrrel?

speaker: TYRREL
text: The chaplain of the Tower hath buried them,

speaker: TYRREL
text: But where, to say the truth, I do not know.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Come to me, Tyrrel, soon at after-supper,

speaker: RICHARD
text: When thou shalt tell the process of their death.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Meantime, but think how I may do thee good,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And be inheritor of thy desire.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Farewell till then.

speaker: TYRREL
text: I humbly take my leave.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The son of Clarence have I pent up close,

speaker: RICHARD
text: His daughter meanly have I matched in marriage,

speaker: RICHARD
text: The sons of Edward sleep in Abraham’s bosom,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And Anne my wife hath bid this world goodnight.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Now, for I know the Breton Richmond aims

speaker: RICHARD
text: At young Elizabeth, my brother’s daughter,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And by that knot looks proudly on the crown,

speaker: RICHARD
text: To her go I, a jolly thriving wooer.

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: My lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Good or bad news, that thou com’st in so bluntly?

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Bad news, my lord. Morton is fled to Richmond,

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: And Buckingham, backed with the hardy Welshmen,

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Is in the field, and still his power increaseth.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ely with Richmond troubles me more near

speaker: RICHARD
text: Than Buckingham and his rash-levied strength.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Come, I have learned that fearful commenting

speaker: RICHARD
text: Is leaden servitor to dull delay;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Delay leads impotent and snail-paced beggary;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then fiery expedition be my wing,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Jove’s Mercury, and herald for a king.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Go, muster men. My counsel is my shield.

speaker: RICHARD
text: We must be brief when traitors brave the field.
